text.title: Vendor Evaluation Framework Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Vendor Evaluation Framework application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Score axes: fit, integrations, pricing, support, security, exit cost. 4-6 axes. Below 4 = thin; above 6 = analysis paralysis.
rule.rationale: Multi-axis decisions outperform single-axis (e.g., 'cheapest'); 4-6 is the productive range.

rule.statement: Run a real-data trial for ≥30 days BEFORE long-term contract. Not pilots, not demos: actual usage.
rule.rationale: Demos hide problems; 30-day trials surface them while reversal is cheap.

rule.statement: Define the rollback gate: 'if X criterion not met by day 60, switch back/elsewhere'. Without rollback, lock-in dominates.
rule.rationale: Vendors design for stickiness; explicit rollback gate is the user's anti-lock-in.

rule.statement: Each axis scored by relevant stakeholder (security by security, support by ops). One-person scoring biases.
rule.rationale: Single-scorer rubrics encode that scorer's bias as decision; multi-stakeholder gives full picture.

rule.statement: Annual revisit: still the right choice? New entrants, price changes, contract renewals. Don't auto-renew without revisit.
rule.rationale: Vendor space shifts annually; auto-renew = silent suboptimal.
